skill.name: error-debugging-error-analysis
skill.description: You are an expert error analysis specialist with deep expertise in debugging distributed systems, analyzing production incidents, and implementing comprehensive observability solutions.
skill.summary: Expert error analysis skill for debugging distributed systems, analyzing production incidents, and implementing observability solutions.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: This is an educational debugging and error analysis skill. Static findings flagged example code patterns in documentation (shell commands, network calls, environment variables) but these are legitimate educational examples for observability implementation. No malicious intent detected. All flagged patterns are standard debugging and logging practices documented for teaching purposes.

medium_findings.title: Documentation Contains Example Shell Commands
medium_findings.description: The implementation playbook contains example shell commands (backtick execution) used in debugging tutorials. These are educational examples, not actual code execution within the skill.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: Backtick commands in documentation are clearly educational examples showing debugging patterns, not runtime code execution

medium_findings.title: Documentation Contains Example Network Calls
medium_findings.description: The skill contains example HTTP client code for observability tool integration (Sentry, DataDog). These are standard integration patterns for error tracking.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: Network calls are example integrations with legitimate observability services, clearly documented as reference implementations

medium_findings.title: Documentation Contains Environment Variable Examples
medium_findings.description: Example code shows environment variable access for configuring observability SDKs. Standard practice for configuration management.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: Environment variables in example code are for SDK configuration (DSN, environment, release), not credential exfiltration

low_findings.title: Static Scanner Flagged System Commands in Debugging Context
low_findings.description: Commands like git, shell operations mentioned in debugging tutorials are legitimate debugging instructions.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: These are documented debugging commands for investigating production issues, standard DevOps practices

content.value_statement: This skill helps developers quickly identify the root cause of errors in production systems and implement proper error handling and observability solutions.
actual_capabilities: Analyze error messages and stack traces to identify root causes
Implement structured logging and correlation ID patterns for distributed systems
Set up error tracking with Sentry, DataDog, and OpenTelemetry
Debug production issues using observability tools and techniques
Implement retry logic, circuit breakers, and graceful degradation
Conduct post-incident analysis and create preventive measures
limitations: Cannot access external systems or run code on user infrastructure
Cannot directly analyze logs without user providing them
Cannot make changes to production systems without user approval
Requires user to provide error context, logs, or traces for analysis

use_cases.title: Production Incident Response
use_cases.description: Analyze production errors during incidents, identify root cause, and implement immediate fixes with rollback plans.
use_cases.target_user: Software engineers and DevOps responding to production incidents

use_cases.title: Debugging Distributed Systems
use_cases.description: Trace errors across microservices using correlation IDs, distributed tracing, and log aggregation.
use_cases.target_user: Backend engineers working with microservices architecture

use_cases.title: Setting Up Observability
use_cases.description: Implement comprehensive error tracking, logging, and monitoring for applications.
use_cases.target_user: Developers building robust error handling and monitoring

prompt_templates.title: Basic Error Analysis
prompt_templates.prompt: Analyze this error message and stack trace. Identify the likely root cause and suggest fixes: $ERROR_MESSAGE
prompt_templates.scenario: User provides error details for analysis

prompt_templates.title: Production Debugging
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me debug a production issue. The error is occurring in $SERVICE_NAME. Here are the relevant logs: $LOGS
prompt_templates.scenario: User needs help debugging a live system issue

prompt_templates.title: Observability Implementation
prompt_templates.prompt: I want to set up error tracking for my $LANGUAGE application. What are the best practices for $TOOL_NAME integration?
prompt_templates.scenario: User wants to implement error tracking

prompt_templates.title: Root Cause Analysis
prompt_templates.prompt: We are experiencing $ERROR_TYPE errors in our system. Walk me through the systematic debugging process to find the root cause.

output_examples.input: Analyze this error: NullPointerException at UserService.java:45
output_examples.output: Based on the stack trace, the NullPointerException originates at UserService.java:45. This typically means a null value was passed to a method that expects a non-null argument. Recommended approach: 1) Check the call chain to find where null is passed, 2) Add null validation at the entry point, 3) Consider using Optional types to make nullability explicit.

output_examples.input: How do I set up distributed tracing for my microservices?
output_examples.output: For distributed tracing, I recommend: 1) Use OpenTelemetry for vendor-neutral instrumentation, 2) Implement correlation IDs in all service headers, 3) Set up a trace collector (Jaeger or Tempo), 4) Add trace context propagation across service boundaries. Here is a code example for middleware...

best_practices: Always reproduce errors in a controlled environment before proposing fixes
Use structured logging with correlation IDs to trace requests across services
Implement error boundaries and graceful degradation to prevent cascading failures
anti_patterns: Ignoring errors without proper logging and alerting
Hardcoding sensitive data in error messages or logs
Catching exceptions without taking action or logging them

faq.question: Can this skill fix errors in my production system?
faq.answer: No, this skill provides analysis and recommendations. It cannot directly modify your code or systems. You must implement any proposed fixes yourself.

faq.question: What information should I provide for error analysis?
faq.answer: Provide the error message, stack trace, relevant log entries, and context about when the error occurs. Include any error patterns you have noticed.

faq.question: Does this skill work with all programming languages?
faq.answer: Yes, the debugging principles apply to any language. Code examples in the skill cover Node.js, Python, Go, Java, and other languages.

faq.question: Can this skill help with performance issues?
faq.answer: Yes, the skill covers memory leaks, performance profiling, and resource exhaustion errors. It includes techniques for debugging slow queries and CPU spikes.

faq.question: How does this skill handle sensitive data in logs?
faq.answer: The skill includes best practices for redacting secrets and PII from logs. It demonstrates how to configure scrubbing in error tracking tools.

faq.question: Is this skill useful for frontend errors?
faq.answer: Yes, it covers frontend debugging with browser DevTools, error boundaries in React, and frontend error tracking with Sentry RUM.
